turns.name: FRANK C. JONES
turns.text: Yes, I'll fake advantage of that oppor­ tunity . Senator Bacon devised his property in trust for the sole and exclusive benefit of the white women and children of the City of Macon. This Court decided in Evans versus Newton that Baconsfield cannot beqperated in accordance with that racial restriction, even by the City of Macon as Trustee, or by private trustees, excepting in acting in accordance with that decision, the Supreme Court of Georgia then held that under Stata Law the sole purpose for which this trust was created, applying several rules of construction under state law had failed. That the trust terminated for that reason without of the state statute providing that whenever a trust fails for any reason, a resulting trust is implied for the benefit of the donor or testator or his heirs because of that statute, again, j having nothing to do with race or discrimination in any respect; this property reverts under state law back to the heirs. A Your Honor, it’s a statute of long vintage. It’s predated to 9 when this will was made, tod for — as far as I know, it has been a majority law for a hundred years j completely consistent with the holding in this Court in Evans versus Newton. We say that no constitutional rights of the Petitioners in this case have been denied and respectfully ask that the Supreme Court of Georgia's decision should he upheld. I'll complete my argument tomorrow»
